mechanic.description: When a player plays a card matching the previously played card value, they gain +2 bonus points, regardless of which player played last.
mechanic.python_code:
import numpy as np

def last_card_surge_symmetric(game_state: dict) -> dict:
    if 'custom_state' not in game_state or not isinstance(game_state['custom_state'], dict):
        game_state['custom_state'] = {}

    last_played = game_state.get('last_played', None)
    if last_played is None:
        return game_state

    current_player = game_state.get('current_player', 1)

    cs = game_state['custom_state']

    # Track last_played value processed for this same already-updated game_state.
    # Also track the previous last_played so we can compare against the immediately prior play.
    prev_last_before = cs.get('last_card_surge_prev_last_played', None)

    # Avoid double-applying on repeated calls with the same last_played.
    # We only allow one application per distinct last_played value per player.
    processed = cs.get('last_card_surge_processed', set())
    if isinstance(processed, list):
        processed = set(processed)

    processed_key = (current_player, last_played)
    if processed_key in processed:
        return game_state

    # Symmetric combo: reward if this play matches the immediately previous played value.
    if prev_last_before is not None and last_played == prev_last_before:
        scores = game_state.get('scores', {})
        if isinstance(scores, dict) and current_player in scores:
            game_state['scores'][current_player] = int(scores[current_player]) + 2

    processed.add(processed_key)
    cs['last_card_surge_processed'] = list(processed)
    # Update previous value to the one just played for the next comparison.
    cs['last_card_surge_prev_last_played'] = last_played

    return game_state
mechanic.justification: This preserves the “last card” combo idea while making the reward symmetric so neither player inherently benefits from turn order.

parent_summary: Baseline game without the new mechanic.
